story_prompt: create a lecture on javascript class

scenes.text: Introduction to JavaScript Classes: A JavaScript class is a template for creating objects. It acts as a blueprint for object instances with properties and methods common to all instances created from the class.
Defining a Class: In JavaScript, classes are defined using the class keyword followed by the class name. Inside the class, a constructor method is used for initializing new objects.
scenes.image_prompt:
classDiagram
  class Car {
    +String model
    +int year
    +constructor(model, year)
    +start()
    +stop()
  }

scenes.text: Creating Instances: Once a class is defined, new instances can be created using the new keyword. Each instance contains the properties and methods defined in the class.
scenes.image_prompt:
flowchart TD
  A[Create Class] --> B[Initialize Instances]
  B --> C[Add Properties to Instances]

scenes.text: Inheritance in JavaScript Classes: Classes can inherit features from other classes. Using the extends keyword, a class can become a child of another class, inheriting its properties and methods.
Using Static Methods: Static methods belong to the class itself rather than instances of the class. They are invoked using the class name rather than instance variables.
